canto_title: The Confession of the Second Flame

scenes.summary: A second flame produces a distorted, metallic sound that catches the travelers' attention.
The flame speaks to Dante, asking for news regarding the peace or war in the Romagna region.
Prompted by Virgil, Dante describes the violent political instability and tyranny in the Romagna territory.
Dante continues with a description of the rivers and cities of the land, ending with a request for the flame to identify itself.
The flame declares that it will speak the truth without fear of infamy since no one returns from the abyss to judge it.
The soul confesses to a past life of cunning and trickery, acting more like a fox than a lion.
The soul recounts how, in his later years, he turned to repentance but remained a master of fraudulent schemes.
The soul describes how he used his cunning to advise the Pope, wielding spiritual power like the keys of heaven.
Realizing the danger of his words, the soul offers a final piece of advice that will lead to his downfall.
A dark cherub prevents Saint Francis from reaching the soul, citing the soul's unrepentant fraud.
Minos, the judge of the underworld, wraps his tail around his body and marks the soul for his specific sin.
The flame departs in agony, and Dante and Virgil continue their journey across the bridge.
